calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action Of Plaintiff Jonathan King As Terminating Sanction For Continued Failure To Respond To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,635.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Christine D. Luong-Pham
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NEW PACIFIC HOTEL INC. DBA HOTEL EPIK's Motion To Dismiss Action Of Plaintiff Jonathan King As Terminating Sanction For Continued Failure To Respond To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,635.00.

The unopposed motion of defendant New Pacific Hotel Inc. for terminating sanctions in view of Plaintiff's failure to respond to discovery is granted. On October 2, 2024, the court ordered Plaintiff to respond to outstanding discovery. The moving papers establish that he has not yet done so and trial is set for May 5, 2025. Monetary sanctions have been ineffective. The court grants the motion for terminating sanctions and dismisses the matter pursuant to CCP 2023.030(d)(3). This is a personal injury action and Defendant cannot reasonably be expected to defend it without discovery from Plaintiff about his claims and damages. In light of its terminating sanction, the court does not order further monetary sanctions to Defendant.

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Nov-06-2024 Text Ruling

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. RAYMOND GHERMEZIAN, Esq. and CORALIA LESIN, Esq. of Raymond Ghermezian, A Professional Law Corporation (Tel: 323-900-5800) for plaintiff;
Atty. KEVIN PLACE, Esq. of McLean Vasquez Place Fabrega (Tel: 949-656-4601) for defendant New Pacific Hotel Inc. dba Hotel Epik.

HEARING RE: MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL AS TO PLAINTIFF JONATHAN KING.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear.

Matter discussed.

The Court rules as follows: Raymond Ghermezian, Esq's motion to be relieved as counsel as to plaintiff Jonathan King is GRANTED.

Movant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order using the mandatory Judicial Council Form.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion To Ompel Responses To Form Nterrogatories, Set 1 And
Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $960.00
Off Calendar Oct-02-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Christine D. Luong-Pham, Esaq., McLEAN VASQUEZ PLACE FABREGA, APC 1(949) 656-4601 Attorney for Defendant New Pacific Hotel Inc. DBA Hotel Epik (erroneously sued as Hotel Epik)
No other appearances

Counsel for Defendant does not contest the Court's tentative ruling and stipulates to Judge Pro Tem Wiliam Lynn decciding this motion.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NEW PACIFIC HOTEL INC. DBA HOTEL EPIK (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS HOTEL EPIK) Motion To Compel Responses To Form Nterrogatories, Set 1 And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $960.00

The three (3) unopposed motions of Defendant New Pacific Hotel Inc. dba Hotel Epik are granted. Monetary sanctions requested are also granted in the amount of $960 for each motion, for the grand total of $2,880. Plaintiff Jonathan King shall serve code-compliant responses without objections and payment of monetary sanctions within 14 days of service of notice of entry of this order.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem williamclynn@gmail.com

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)
